axis_responsibility.Agent: This return is design-lead support evidence only and does not classify success, quality, or route.
axis_responsibility.Brick: The task and Building Plan declaration define the work and graph edges; declared terminal graph edges are Brick work composition evidence, not closure execution.
axis_responsibility.Evidence: raw/link.jsonl rows are observed evidence; raw:link-graph:* rows are declaration records, while raw:link:* closed-boundary rows are executed Link evidence for this predicate.
axis_responsibility.Link: Closed-boundary Movement meaning remains Link-owned; the observer may read recorded Link evidence but must not choose Movement, target, route, or sufficiency.
axis_responsibility.Support: frontier_observation, ledger/dashboard projection, and checker fixtures are support mechanics that read evidence and expose the bug/regression pin.

candidate_file_changes: support/checkers/profiles/read_side_projection_boundary.yaml only if a new kernel check id or explicit text pin is added; not needed if dashboard_productization_projection is extended in place
support/checkers/check_profile.py only if a new kernel check id is introduced; prefer avoiding this
checker_or_verifier_plan: Verifier-first: patch or extend support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py before or with the code change, preferably inside run_dashboard_productization_projection already covered by read_side_projection_boundary.
Negative pin: fixture with raw/link.jsonl terminal raw:link-graph:* closed-boundary row and no raw:link:* closed-boundary row must observe closure_pending/non-closed projection.
Positive pin: fixture with raw:link:* closed-boundary row and building_lifecycle_state closed must observe complete/closed projection.

Mutation RED: monkeypatch the closure predicate to ignore raw_ref namespace, or to use the current target-only body; the declared graph fixture must become closed and _dashboard_state_assert_expected must reject it.
Branch-preservation probe: keep existing projection-parked and projection-adapter-error cases unchanged so chat_session_parked and agent_incomplete precedence remains pinned.

Post-implementation commands to run in a temp source copy: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=support/import_identity python3 support/checkers/check_profile.py --all; plus direct observe_building_frontier probes for the temp declared-edge fixture, the real notify-v2 root if present in that copy, and f9-projection-states-0612.
returned.design_summary: Repair should make closure observation depend on executed Link evidence, not declared graph plan-edge evidence. The low-blast-radius design is to extract one private predicate, for example _is_executed_closed_boundary_record(record), and use it from both _closed_boundary_raw_record_after_latest_pause and _closed_boundary_observed. The predicate should require raw_ref.startswith('raw:link:') plus _is_closed_boundary_ref(target). This mirrors the already-correct sibling helper and excludes raw:link-graph:* and raw:link-frontier:* without relying on optional boolean annotations. The checker fixture should also keep an executed closure row carrying building_lifecycle_state == 'closed' so lifecycle remains observed evidence, even if raw_ref namespace is the predicate gate.
edge_cases: A declared graph row missing declared_graph_edge but still using raw:link-graph:* must remain non-closing.
A record with raw_ref raw:link-frontier:* and closed-looking text must remain non-closing.
A non-boundary target that merely ends with closed must remain non-closing through _is_closed_boundary_ref.
A paused/resumed path must use the same executed-record predicate after latest pause, preventing helper drift.
A building with agent_received_records greater than agent_return_records must continue to report agent_incomplete before the fallback, even if declared terminal graph rows exist.
Synthetic checker fixtures should include building_lifecycle_state closed on executed closure rows to keep fixture shape close to real ledger evidence.
invariants: A declared graph terminal edge alone must never produce frontier_kind complete.
An executed raw:link:* record targeting a closed building boundary must still produce complete.
raw:link-frontier:* records must not be accepted as executed closure records.
declared_graph_edge truthy rows must be ignored for closure even if their target is a closed boundary.
The fallback must not inspect process liveness or infer provider state.
Checker green, dashboard rendering, and projection freshness remain support evidence only.
not_proven: No repository repair was made in this read-only design pass.
Temp drive before/after fixture proof was not run because this Agent instruction forbids creating or writing files.
The real notify-v2-vessel-guard-voice ledger could not be observed in this worktree because that root was absent.
Mutation RED is not proven until the checker fixture is implemented and run.
Full check_profile.py --all in a temp source copy was not run.
Complete absence of future support-authority leaks is not proven.
No source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, or Movement authority is claimed.
observed_evidence: Read-only inspection only; no files were edited, created, deleted, committed, pushed, or written.
support/operator/frontier_observation.py:83-105 shows S1 park, agent_incomplete, pause-after-close, paused, and waiting branches all precede the no-pause fallback at line 105.
support/operator/frontier_observation.py:173-189 has the stricter sibling helper discipline: it ignores records whose raw_ref does not start with raw:link:.
support/operator/frontier_observation.py:216-237 shows _closed_boundary_observed currently accepts any closed-boundary target and does not filter declared graph rows.

In-memory probe observed declared_graph raw_ref raw:link-graph:* with target building-boundary:x-closed returns True from _closed_boundary_observed and False from _closed_boundary_raw_record_after_latest_pause; executed raw_ref raw:link:03 closure returns True from both.

project/brick-protocol/buildings/f9-projection-states-0612/raw/link.jsonl:3 is an executed closure row with raw_ref raw:link:03, building_lifecycle_state closed, and target building-boundary:f9-projection-states-0612-closed; observe_building_frontier reports complete for that root.

project/brick-protocol/buildings/adapter-30-s1-park/raw/link.jsonl:2-9 shows declared graph rows use declared_graph_edge true and raw_ref raw:link-graph:*; line 9 is the terminal declared closed-boundary edge. Its current frontier is agent_incomplete because that branch precedes fallback, so it is not a no-pause fallback proof.
project/brick-protocol/buildings/notify-v2-vessel-guard-voice was not present under this worktree's project/brick-protocol/buildings path.
No Claude/Gemini/model review was run in this read-only design pass.

proposed_changes: In support/operator/frontier_observation.py, add a single private helper that checks executed closed-boundary Link records and have both closure helpers call it.

Use raw_ref namespace as the structural discriminator: include raw:link: executed rows; exclude raw:link-graph:* declared graph rows and raw:link-frontier:* observation rows; then require _is_closed_boundary_ref(target).
Keep branch order above the fallback byte-identical; do not move chat_session_parked, agent_incomplete, link_paused, human_review_waiting, or pause-after-close branches.

In support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py, extend dashboard_productization_projection state fixtures with a no-pause graph-declared-terminal-only case: agent returned evidence present, raw/link.jsonl contains a raw:link-graph:* terminal closed-boundary row, no executed closure row, expected frontier_kind closure_pending and dashboard disp running/non-closed.
Add or update the executed-closure fixture so a raw:link:* closed-boundary row with building_lifecycle_state closed remains complete.
Add a mutation probe that replaces the fixed predicate with the current broken behavior, proving the declared graph terminal fixture turns closed and the checker fires RED.
relevant_current_structure: support/operator/frontier_observation.py owns static frontier observation and states it is not process liveness, source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, or Movement authority.
observe_building_frontier initializes closure_pending, then only switches to complete through closed_boundary_after_latest_pause or the no-pause _closed_boundary_observed fallback.
support/operator/plan_graph.py:576-583 defines declared graph raw refs as raw:link-graph:*; these are declaration evidence, not execution closure evidence.
support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py:3405-3431 defines dashboard state projection cases including projection-mid-walk as closure_pending/running and projection-closed as complete/closed.

support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py:3503-3552 already has a RED probe for accidentally deriving closure from building_map.link_edges, but it does not pin raw/link.jsonl declared graph terminal rows.

support/checkers/profiles/read_side_projection_boundary.yaml:4-10 already includes dashboard_productization_projection, so the F13 checker pin can extend that existing family without opening a new standalone checker.
returned.returned_summary: local CLI Agent Adapter returned support evidence
unchanged_surfaces: No link/, agent/, brick/, or project/ edits are needed for the repair surface.
No scheduler, queue, retry, timer, provider runtime, dashboard runtime, Slack/webhook/thread delivery, route selection, or Movement choice should be added.
No GateFact, Agent success/failure field, quality verdict, or source-truth surface should be introduced.
Existing S1 park, S2 submit, chat_session_parked, agent_incomplete, and link_paused branch behavior should remain unchanged because the edit is only inside closure-record recognition.
Existing dashboard display mapping can stay unchanged; the input frontier_kind should be corrected before projection.
